title: Can Puck reinvent the news business for the influencer age?
author: Nilay Patel

content.full_text:
Can Puck reinvent the news business for the influencer age? Today, I’m talking with Sarah Personette, CEO of Puck, which is a fancy new media company that’s been around for just about five years now. Puck hires big stars and gives them newsletters that are all mostly part of a subscription bundle. These newsletters ar…
CEO Sarah Personettes big bet on the place where influencers and reporters might meet by Nilay PatelClose Posts from this author will be added to your daily e…

ai_output.summary: Puck is a media company founded about five years ago that focuses on hiring prominent figures to create newsletters, primarily offered through a subscription bundle. CEO Sarah Personette aims to blend the worlds of influencers and traditional reporters through this model. The article explores whether Puck can reinvent the news business for the influencer era.

title: Did Neuralink make the wrong bet?
author: Elissa Welle

content.full_text:
Did Neuralink make the wrong bet? Elon Musk promised Neuralink would bring superhuman abilities and minds merged with AI. Then he fueled a runaway hype train for his brain implant technology, which ended up with a grisly record for implants in monkeys and some success with human subjects. But…
The field of brain-computer interfaces is moving away from mind-controlled computer cursors to restoring speech. by Elissa WelleClose Posts from this author will be ad…

ai_output.summary: Elon Musk's Neuralink initially promised superhuman abilities and AI-enhanced minds but has faced criticism for a poor track record with monkey implants despite some human progress. Meanwhile, the brain-computer interface field is shifting focus from mind-controlled cursors toward restoring speech functions.

title: From LLMs to hallucinations, here's a simple guide to common AI terms | TechCrunch

content.full_text: From LLMs to hallucinations, here's a simple guide to common AI terms | TechCrunch The rise of AI has brought an avalanche of new terms and slang. The scientists who work in this field often rely on jargon and lingo to explain what theyre working on. As a result, we frequently have to use …

ai_output.summary: The article from TechCrunch offers a simple glossary explaining common terms and slang related to artificial intelligence, such as LLMs and hallucinations. It aims to clarify the complex jargon used by AI researchers to help readers better understand the rapidly evolving AI landscape.

ai_output.summary: Anthropic has blocked Claude Pro and Max subscribers from using their flat-rate plans with third-party AI agent frameworks, beginning with OpenClaw. This change, effective from April 4, 2026, transfers the cost of running autonomous agents onto the users.

title: New MiniMax M2.7 proprietary AI model is 'self-evolving' and can perform 30-50% of reinforcement learning research workflow | VentureBeat

content.full_text: New MiniMax M2.7 proprietary AI model is 'self-evolving' and can perform 30-50% of reinforcement learning research workflow | VentureBeat That makes M2.7 one of the most affordable frontier AI models to run in the world — only xAI's Grok 4.1 Fast is cheaper. ai_output.summary: The new MiniMax M2.7 is a proprietary AI model that is 'self-evolving' and can handle 30-50% of the reinforcement learning research workflow. It is among the most affordable frontier AI models globally, with only xAI's Grok 4.1 Fast being cheaper. Chinese startup MiniMax has gained recognition for delivering advanced large language models in the competitive AI market.
